title: Introduction to Programming
description: Introduction to programming using Python. Students complete weekly homework assignments covering basic data types, control flow, functions, and file I/O. Two midterm exams and a final exam are required.

title: Data Structures and Algorithms 1
description: Covers fundamental data structures: linked lists, stacks, queues, trees, and hash tables. Weekly programming assignments, two midterms, and a final exam. Students implement algorithms from scratch in Java.

title: Computer Systems and Organization
description: Low-level computing including assembly language, memory management, and operating system concepts. Heavy weekly lab assignments, multiple exams, and a substantial semester project.

title: Algorithms
description: Rigorous analysis of algorithms and complexity theory. Weekly problem sets, a midterm, and a final exam. Course demands strong mathematical background and extensive written proofs.

title: Computer Networks
description: Study of network protocols, architecture, and socket programming. Several programming projects including implementing TCP over UDP, weekly homework, two exams.

title: Machine Learning
description: Introduction to supervised and unsupervised learning. Weekly programming assignments using scikit-learn and PyTorch. Midterm exam, final project with paper, and weekly quizzes.

title: Digital Logic Design
description: Fundamentals of digital circuits and Boolean algebra. Lab assignments every two weeks, weekly homework, midterm and final exam.

title: Signals and Systems
description: Mathematical treatment of continuous and discrete-time signals. Weekly problem sets, two midterm exams, and a comprehensive final. Demanding mathematical content.

title: Digital Signal Processing
description: Advanced signal processing theory and applications. Weekly problem sets, MATLAB assignments, two exams, and a design project.

title: Calculus I
description: Introduction to differential calculus. Weekly homework assignments, two midterm exams and a final. Covers limits, derivatives, and applications.

title: Calculus II
description: Integral calculus and infinite series. Weekly homework, quizzes every two weeks, two midterms and a final. Faster pace than Calculus I.

title: Elementary Linear Algebra
description: Abstract linear algebra with proofs. Weekly homework assignments, two midterms and a final. Emphasis on theoretical reasoning.

title: Introduction to Real Analysis
description: Rigorous treatment of calculus including epsilon-delta proofs. Weekly proof-based homework, two midterms, final exam. Considered one of the most challenging undergraduate math courses.

title: Real Analysis
description: Graduate-level real analysis covering measure theory and functional analysis. Weekly problem sets with in-depth proofs, midterm and final exam.

title: General Chemistry I
description: Introduction to chemistry including atomic structure and stoichiometry. Weekly problem sets, lab reports, two midterms and a final.

title: Organic Chemistry I
description: Mechanistic organic chemistry. Notorious for demanding workload: weekly homework, lab reports every week, two midterms and a final. Lab takes 3 hours per session.

title: Cell Biology
description: Molecular and cellular biology concepts. Weekly reading assignments, two exams, lab reports, and a final exam.

title: Genetics
description: Classical and molecular genetics. Weekly problem sets, two midterms, final, and a research paper.

title: General Physics I
description: Mechanics and thermodynamics. Weekly homework, recitation quizzes, two midterms and a final. Lab each week with report due.

title: General Physics II
description: Electricity, magnetism, and optics. Weekly homework, lab reports, two midterms and a cumulative final.

title: Principles of Microeconomics
description: Introduction to microeconomic theory and markets. Three exams and weekly reading; minimal homework assignments.

title: Intermediate Microeconomics
description: Mathematical treatment of consumer and producer theory. Weekly problem sets, two midterms and a final.

title: Developmental Psychology
description: Survey of human development from birth through adulthood. Two short papers, two exams, and weekly reading quizzes.

title: Research Methods in Psychology
description: Statistical methods and experimental design. Weekly assignments, midterm, final, and a research project with written report.

title: History of the United States I
description: Survey of American history to the Civil War. Three essays, two exams, and weekly reading.

title: Modern Europe
description: Advanced reading-intensive seminar. Weekly response papers, a long research paper, and participation in discussion.

title: Writing and Critical Inquiry
description: Introduction to academic writing. Four major essays and multiple drafts required. Weekly writing workshops.

title: American Literature
description: Close reading and analysis of canonical American texts. Weekly responses, two essays, midterm and final.

title: Science Technology and Society
description: Introductory survey of how technology shapes society. Two short papers, a midterm, and a final. Minimal homework.

title: Ethics of Artificial Intelligence
description: Seminar discussion of AI ethics. Weekly one-page responses, midterm essay, and a final research paper.

title: Swimming for Fitness
description: Beginner swimming course. Pass/fail. Attendance is the primary requirement; no exams or written assignments.

title: Exercise Physiology
description: Physiology of physical activity and exercise. Lab assignments, two midterms, final, and a fitness project.

title: Concert Band
description: Performance ensemble. Weekly rehearsals and concerts. Graded on attendance and performance quality.

title: Introduction to Drawing
description: Studio art course focused on observational drawing. Weekly studio assignments and a portfolio review at end of semester.

title: Advanced Painting
description: Upper-division studio art. Weekly critiques, project proposals, and a final portfolio. Requires significant out-of-class studio time.

title: Foundations of Community Health
description: Introduction to public health nursing concepts. Two exams, short weekly reflections, and a community assessment project.

title: Clinical Practicum
description: Advanced clinical nursing practice. Extensive clinical hours, weekly reflective journals, case study presentations, and a comprehensive final portfolio.

title: Introduction to Mathematical Statistics
description: Probability theory, distributions, and inference. Weekly problem sets, two midterms and a final exam.

title: Mathematical Statistics
description: Advanced statistical theory and estimation. Weekly problem sets with proofs, midterm and final exam.

title: Probability
description: Applied probability theory for engineers. Weekly homework, two midterms and a final exam.

title: Mathematics of Machine Learning
description: Linear algebra and optimization for machine learning. Demanding weekly problem sets, midterm and final exam, and a project.

title: Linear Statistical Models
description: Statistical modeling and regression analysis. Weekly homework assignments, two exams, and a substantial data analysis project.

title: Introduction to Media Studies
description: Survey of mass media history and theory. Two exams and a short paper; minimal weekly work.

title: Journalism and Society
description: Reading-heavy seminar with weekly reaction posts, a midterm exam, and a final investigative journalism project.

title: Introduction to Philosophy
description: Survey of major philosophical problems and thinkers. Weekly readings, two take-home essays, and a final exam.

title: Epistemology
description: Advanced seminar on theories of knowledge. Weekly response papers, a midterm essay, and a long final paper.
